title: 9 Best Herbal Teas For Hyperthyroidism
intro: Herbal teas for Hyperthyroidism are natural remedies that help alleviate the symptoms of an overactive thyroid gland, characterized by excessive production of thyroid hormones. These teas can be effective in treating hyperthyroidism as they contain bioactive compounds that help regulate thyroid function and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and nervousness. Some herbal teas that can help treat hyperthyroidism include Peppermint tea, which helps calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety, Ashwagandha tea, which acts as an adaptogen to balance thyroid hormones, and Licorice root tea, which helps reduce inflammation and promote healing. Additionally, other herbal teas such as Dandelion root tea, Burdock root tea, and Red clover tea may also be beneficial in treating hyperthyroidism due to their ability to detoxify the body, reduce inflammation, and support thyroid function.

preparations.herb_desc: Astragalus membranaceus teas have been traditionally used to treat hyperthyroidism due to their adaptogenic properties, which help the body to adapt to stress and balance the thyroid function. The bioactive constituents of Astragalus membranaceus, including polysaccharides, flavonoids, and saponins, have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to regulate the thyroid hormones and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Astragalus membranaceus teas can help to slow down the production of thyroid hormones and promote a more balanced thyroid function. The benefits of using Astragalus membranaceus teas to treat hyperthyroidism include reduced symptoms, improved thyroid function, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Astragalus membranaceus root, 1 cup of water, and a tea infuser.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Astragalus membranaceus root and put it in the tea infuser.
Heat the water in a pot until it reaches 212°F (100°C).
Steep the Astragalus membranaceus root in the hot water for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Astragalus membranaceus teas can cause interactions with medications such as blood thinners, thyroid medications, and diabetes medications when used to treat hyperthyroidism, potentially leading to adverse effects such as irregular heartbeat, excessive bleeding, or blood sugar fluctuations. Precautions should be taken when using Astragalus membranaceus teas for hyperthyroidism, including monitoring thyroid hormone levels, blood sugar levels, and blood pressure, and being cautious with its use in pregnant or breastfeeding women.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Astragalus membranaceus for hyperthyroidism treatment.
Drink 1 cup of Astragalus membranaceus tea 2-3 times a day.
Use 1 teaspoon of dried Astragalus membranaceus per cup of boiling water.
Continue treatment for 4-6 weeks under doctor's supervision.
Monitor thyroid function and adjust dosage as advised by your doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been traditionally used to treat hyperthyroidism, a condition characterized by an overactive thyroid gland, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The saponins, flavonoids, and glycosides present in this herbal preparation help to reduce thyroid hormone production, alleviate symptoms such as anxiety and weight loss, and promote a sense of calmness. The bioactive constituents of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, including glycyrrhizin and licorice acid, have been shown to inhibit 11β-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type 1, an enzyme involved in the regulation of cortisol and thyroid hormone levels. Regular consumption of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas has been found to provide relief from hyperthyroidism symptoms, promote overall well-being, and support the body's natural balance.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots.
Crush 1-2 tablespoons of the roots into small pieces.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the crushed roots to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1 cup, 2-3 times a day, after consulting a doctor.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat hyperthyroidism, but possible side effects include potassium loss, water retention, and elevated blood pressure due to its licorice root content. Precautions should be taken when using these teas, such as monitoring blood pressure and potassium levels, limiting the duration of use, and being cautious of interactions with other medications that may exacerbate these issues.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Glycyrrhiza glabra tea for hyperthyroidism.
Take 1-2 teaspoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root in 1 cup of boiling water.
Drink 1-2 cups of tea, 2-3 times a day, for 3-6 months.
Monitor thyroid hormone levels and adjust dosage based on doctor's advice.
Combine with other treatments, such as medication or surgery, as directed.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been traditionally used to treat hyperthyroidism due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents, including gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are often associated with hyperthyroidism. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, ginger tea may help to alleviate symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and anxiety, making it a potential complementary treatment for this condition. The benefits of using ginger tea to treat hyperthyroidism include reduced medication reliance and a decrease in the risk of thyroid hormone imbalances.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh ginger root, a tea infuser, and a cup of boiling water.
Peel the ginger root and slice it thinly. Add the sliced ginger to the tea infuser.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the ginger in the tea infuser. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the ginger. Add 1 tablespoon of honey to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, to help manage hyperthyroidism symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat hyperthyroidism, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its ability to stimulate digestion and increase gut motility. To minimize potential side effects, it's recommended to start with small doses and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming Zingiber officinale teas on an empty stomach or with other stimulants.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 3 times a week.
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried ginger per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink.
Take 1-2 cups daily for 2-3 months for optimal benefits.
Consult a doctor before using ginger tea for hyperthyroidism.

preparations.herb_desc: Taraxacum officinale teas, made from the dandelion plant, have been traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism, a condition characterized by an overactive thyroid gland. The diuretic and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce thyroid hormone levels and alleviate symptoms such as weight loss, palpitations, and anxiety. The bioactive constituents of Taraxacum officinale teas, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, are responsible for its therapeutic effects, which help to regulate thyroid function and reduce oxidative stress. The benefits of using Taraxacum officinale teas to treat hyperthyroidism include a reduction in thyroid hormone levels, improved thyroid function, and alleviation of symptoms, making it a potential natural remedy for managing this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers, or 1 tablespoon of dried herbs.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it starts boiling.
Add the Taraxacum officinale herbs to the boiling water and reduce heat to a simmer.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes, then strain the liquid into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage hyperthyroidism symptoms, but consult a doctor first.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Taraxacum officinale teas can be used to treat hyperthyroidism, but possible side effects may include dizziness, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation, such as monitoring blood pressure and thyroid hormone levels, and being cautious with interactions with other medications, especially blood thinners and diabetes medications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Taraxacum officinale tea 3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase to 2 teaspoons per cup after 3 days.
Continue for 2 weeks, then reduce to 1 teaspoon per cup.
Consult a doctor for further guidance and monitoring.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas, derived from the flowers of the lavender plant, have been traditionally used to treat hyperthyroidism, a condition characterized by an overactive thyroid gland. The calming and soothing properties of these teas help to alleviate symptoms such as anxiety, insomnia, and rapid heartbeat, which are commonly associated with hyperthyroidism. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, including linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to regulate thyroid function and reduce oxidative stress. Regular consumption of Lavandula angustifolia teas may help to normalize thyroid hormone levels, alleviate symptoms, and promote overall well-being, making it a potential natural remedy for managing hyperthyroidism.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers. You can buy this at a health food store or online.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers and place them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool for 1-2 minutes. The ideal temperature for tea is between 95°F to 104°F.
Pour the boiling water over the Lavandula angustifolia flowers in the tea infuser or cup. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it hot or cold. You can add honey to taste, but be sure to consult with a doctor before consuming it for hyperthyroidism.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to treat hyperthyroidism, but its use may result in side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and nausea due to its potential to affect the thyroid gland's function and interact with thyroid medications. Precautions should be taken when using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat hyperthyroidism, including monitoring thyroid hormone levels and adjusting the dosage to avoid exacerbating symptoms like anxiety, insomnia, and rapid heartbeat.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Lavandula angustifolia tea for hyperthyroidism.
Take 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia tea 2-3 times a day.
Use 1 tablespoon of dried flowers for every cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Continue for 2-4 weeks, then monitor thyroid hormone levels.

preparations.herb_desc: Foeniculum vulgare teas, also known as anise or fennel tea, have been traditionally used to treat hyperthyroidism due to their anti-inflammatory and antithyroid properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by regulating thyroid hormone production, reducing thyroid gland activity, and alleviating symptoms such as weight loss and palpitations. The bioactive constituents of fennel tea, including anethole, fenchone, and limonene, are responsible for its therapeutic effects, as they have been shown to inhibit the production of thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) and reduce the expression of thyroid-related genes. Regular consumption of Foeniculum vulgare teas has been found to provide benefits in managing hyperthyroidism, including improved thyroid function, reduced anxiety and tremors, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Foeniculum vulgare (also known as fennel) leaves and 1 cup of water.
Chop the fennel leaves and place them in a saucepan.
Add 1 cup of water to the saucepan and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat and let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and drink 1 cup of the fennel tea 2-3 times a day to help manage hyperthyroidism symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Foeniculum vulgare teas can be used to treat hyperthyroidism, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive digestive systems. Precautions should be taken to avoid interactions with medications, such as blood thinners, and to monitor blood sugar levels, as Foeniculum vulgare may affect blood sugar control.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Foeniculum vulgare tea for hyperthyroidism.
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried Foeniculum vulgare leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard solids.
Drink 1-2 cups of Foeniculum vulgare tea 2-3 times a day.
Maintain dosage for 4-6 weeks under doctor's supervision and monitoring.

preparations.herb_desc: Panax ginseng teas have been studied for their potential in treating hyperthyroidism, an overactive thyroid condition, by modulating the immune system and regulating thyroid hormone production. The bioactive constituents, including ginsenosides, panaxynol, and panaxydol, help to reduce inflammation and normalize thyroid function. By inhibiting the release of thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) and reducing thyroid hormone levels, Panax ginseng teas may provide relief from hyperthyroidism symptoms such as weight loss, anxiety, and tremors. The benefits of using Panax ginseng teas to treat hyperthyroidism include reduced thyroid hormone levels, improved immune function, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of water and 2 teaspoons of dried Panax ginseng root in a saucepan.
Bring the water to a boil on medium heat, then reduce the heat to low.
Add the dried Panax ginseng root to the water and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the root.
Drink 1 cup of the Panax ginseng tea 2-3 times a day to help manage hyperthyroidism symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "International archives of allergy and applied immunology", Panax ginseng teas may be beneficial for hyperthyroidism due to their ability to inhibit the transformation of peripheral blood lymphocytes, suggesting a potential immunomodulatory effect.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Panax ginseng teas can be used to treat hyperthyroidism, but possible side effects include insomnia, anxiety, and increased heart rate due to its stimulatory effect, and it may also interact with blood thinners and other medications. Precautions to take when using Panax ginseng teas include monitoring blood pressure and heart rate, as well as being cautious of its potential interaction with other medications, especially blood thinners, and using it in moderation to avoid exacerbating the condition.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Panax ginseng tea for hyperthyroidism treatment.
Start with 1 teaspoon of Panax ginseng powder per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes and strain the tea. Drink 2-3 cups daily.
Begin with low doses (1 teaspoon) and gradually increase up to 2-3 teaspoons.
Monitor thyroid hormone levels and adjust dosages as recommended by doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas have been used for centuries to treat hyperthyroidism, a condition characterized by an overactive thyroid gland. The antimalarial properties of Cinchona officinalis teas, specifically the presence of quinine and other alkaloids, help to regulate thyroid function and reduce symptoms such as rapid heartbeat and weight loss. The bioactive constituents of Cinchona officinalis teas, including quinine and cinchonine, interact with the thyroid gland to slow down its activity, thereby alleviating hyperthyroidism symptoms. The benefits of using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat hyperthyroidism include reduced thyroid hormone production, improved cardiovascular health, and a decrease in anxiety and nervousness associated with the condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Cinchona officinalis bark, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser.
Place the Cinchona officinalis bark in the tea infuser.
Pour the boiling water over the bark in the infuser.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then remove the infuser.
Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed to help manage hyperthyroidism symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can potentially treat hyperthyroidism, but using them may lead to side effects such as nausea, dizziness, and irregular heartbeat due to the presence of quinine and other compounds that can interact with thyroid medications. It is crucial to use these teas with caution, as excessive consumption can cause vomiting, diarrhea, and even seizures in rare cases, and to avoid consuming them in combination with other medications that may increase their toxic effects.
preparation_usage: Start with 1/2 teaspoon of Cinchona officinalis powder in 1 cup of boiling water daily.
Take 2-3 cups of Cinchona officinalis tea in a day, divided into 3-4 portions.
Drink tea 2-3 hours after meals to avoid interactions with other medications.
Gradually increase the dosage to 1 teaspoon per 1 cup of water over 7-10 days.
Consult a healthcare professional for guidance on treatment duration and dosage.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas, also known as milk thistle tea, have been used in traditional medicine to treat hyperthyroidism due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including flavonoids and silymarin, help to inhibit the conversion of T4 to T3, thereby reducing the levels of thyroid hormones in the body. Regular consumption of Silybum marianum teas may help to alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism, such as weight loss, tremors, and palpitations, by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the thyroid gland. The benefits of using Silybum marianum teas to treat hyperthyroidism include reduced risk of thyroid hormone imbalance, improved thyroid function, and a decrease in the severity of symptoms associated with this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather Silybum marianum flowers and leaves. Use 1 cup of fresh or dried plants.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add 1 cup of Silybum marianum flowers and leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1/2 cup, 2-3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used to treat hyperthyroidism, but its use may lead to side effects such as gastrointestinal upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation, including monitoring for interactions with other medications and being aware of potential allergic reactions, especially when consuming large quantities or for extended periods.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Silybum marianum tea for hyperthyroidism.
Drink 1 cup of Silybum marianum tea 3 times a day, after meals.
Start with 1/2 cup of tea for the first week, then increase to 1 cup.
Take Silybum marianum tea for 2-3 months, under doctor's supervision.
Monitor thyroid hormone levels and adjust treatment as advised by the doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Lycium barbarum teas, also known as Goji tea, have been traditionally used to treat hyperthyroidism, a condition characterized by an overactive thyroid gland. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce thyroid hormone production, alleviate symptoms such as anxiety and tremors, and promote overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Lycium barbarum tea, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects, inhibiting the activity of thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) and reducing thyroid hormone levels. Regular consumption of Lycium barbarum tea has been reported to help manage hyperthyroidism symptoms, improve quality of life, and reduce the need for pharmaceutical medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Lycium barbarum berries and 2 cups of water.
Rinse the berries and remove stems. Chop them into small pieces.
Combine the chopped berries and water in a saucepan. Bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 15-20 minutes. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, after meals to help manage hyperthyroidism symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lycium barbarum teas can be used to treat hyperthyroidism, but possible side effects may include dizziness, nausea, and fatigue due to the potential interference with thyroid hormone regulation. Precautions to take when using Lycium barbarum teas for this purpose include monitoring blood sugar levels and thyroid hormone levels, as well as being aware of potential interactions with medications such as blood thinners and diabetes medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Paeonia lactiflora teas have been traditionally used as a natural remedy to treat hyperthyroidism, a condition characterized by an overactive thyroid gland. The antithyroid properties of Paeonia lactiflora teas help to reduce the production of thyroid hormones, thereby alleviating the symptoms of hyperthyroidism. The bioactive constituents of Paeonia lactiflora teas, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, have been shown to inhibit the activity of thyroid peroxidase, an enzyme essential for thyroid hormone production. The benefits of using Paeonia lactiflora teas to treat hyperthyroidism include reduced symptoms such as weight loss, anxiety, and rapid heartbeat, as well as improved overall thyroid function and well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Paeonia lactiflora roots (or 2 teaspoons of dried roots).
Wash the roots with water, then chop them into small pieces.
Combine 1 cup of water and the chopped roots in a saucepan. Heat over medium heat.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the roots. Drink 1/2 cup, 2-3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Paeonia lactiflora teas for hyperthyroidism have not been mentioned in the provided study, as the study focuses on the traditional uses, phytochemistry, and pharmacological activities of the genus Paeonia, but does not specifically mention the treatment of hyperthyroidism.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Paeonia lactiflora teas can be used as a potential treatment for hyperthyroidism, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals, as well as potentially interacting with medications like blood thinners and thyroid medications. Precautions when using Paeonia lactiflora teas to treat hyperthyroidism include starting with low doses and gradually increasing the amount, avoiding consumption with other medications, and monitoring blood pressure and thyroid hormone levels regularly.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas have been studied for their potential in treating hyperthyroidism due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms. The bioactive constituents of Curcuma longa, including curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, have been shown to inhibit the activity of thyroid peroxidase, a key enzyme involved in thyroid hormone synthesis. The consumption of Curcuma longa teas has been associated with reduced levels of thyroid hormones and improved symptoms in individuals with hyperthyroidism, highlighting its potential as a complementary treatment option. Overall, the benefits of using Curcuma longa teas to treat hyperthyroidism include reduced thyroid hormone levels, alleviated symptoms, and a lower risk of adverse effects compared to conventional medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of water and 1 teaspoon of dried Curcuma longa root.
Boil the water in a pot and reduce heat to low.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried Curcuma longa root to the pot and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the root.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, before meals, to help manage hyperthyroidism symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Current medicinal chemistry, Curcuma longa teas for hyperthyroidism may be beneficial due to the anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, anti-proliferative, apoptosis, angiogenesis, cell cycle, and metastasis effects of curcumin.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be beneficial in treating hyperthyroidism, but it may cause side effects such as digestive issues, nausea, and stomach ulcers due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Precautions should be taken to avoid interactions with other medications, such as blood thinners, and to monitor liver function, as excessive consumption of Curcuma longa teas may lead to liver damage.

preparations.herb_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat hyperthyroidism, a condition characterized by an overactive thyroid gland. The sedative and antispasmodic properties of Valeriana officinalis teas help to reduce anxiety, stress, and tremors associated with hyperthyroidism, promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system. The bioactive constituents of Valeriana officinalis, including valepotriates, isovaltrate, and valerenic acid, contribute to its therapeutic effects by modulating the thyroid hormone levels and inhibiting the production of thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH). By using Valeriana officinalis teas, individuals with hyperthyroidism may experience reduced symptoms, improved sleep quality, and overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Valeriana officinalis root and 1 quart of water in a large pot.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried Valeriana officinalis root to 1 cup of boiling water in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the root in boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup.
Drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day, ideally 30 minutes before meals, to help manage hyperthyroidism symptoms.
Consult a healthcare professional before using Valeriana officinalis tea as a treatment for hyperthyroidism, as it may interact with medications or worsen certain conditions.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Valeriana officinalis teas can be used to treat hyperthyroidism, but may cause side effects such as drowsiness, stomach upset, and interactions with other medications like blood thinners, which can increase the risk of bleeding. preparations.herb_desc: Schisandra chinensis teas have been traditionally used to treat hyperthyroidism, a condition characterized by an overactive thyroid gland. The properties of this herbal preparation help to regulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms such as weight loss, rapid heartbeat, and anxiety. The bioactive constituents of Schisandra chinensis, including schisandrins, lignans, and polysaccharides, are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects by modulating the immune system and reducing inflammation in the thyroid gland. Drinking Schisandra chinensis teas may help to normalize thyroid hormone levels, reduce oxidative stress, and promote overall well-being in individuals with hyperthyroidism.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Schisandra chinensis berries and 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure out 1 tablespoon of dried Schisandra chinensis berries and add them to a teapot or infuser.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Schisandra chinensis teas can be used to treat hyperthyroidism, but it may cause side effects such as liver damage, stomach upset, and allergic reactions, especially when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. To use Schisandra chinensis teas safely, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase the amount, avoid consuming it with other medications or supplements that may interact with it, and monitor your body's response to prevent any adverse effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Angelica sinensis teas, also known as dong quai, have been traditionally used to treat hyperthyroidism due to their unique properties that help to regulate thyroid function. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, including ferulic acid, isoflavones, and coumarins, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help to reduce thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism. The herbal preparation of Angelica sinensis teas helps to treat hyperthyroidism by modulating the immune system, reducing oxidative stress, and inhibiting the activity of thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH). The benefits of using Angelica sinensis teas to treat hyperthyroidism include improved thyroid function, reduced symptoms of hyperthyroidism, and a decrease in the risk of thyroid-related complications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Angelica sinensis root, a tea infuser, and 1 cup of boiling water.
Place the Angelica sinensis root in the tea infuser and steep it in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the Angelica sinensis root.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, and stir well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally before meals, to help manage hyperthyroidism symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Angelica sinensis teas can be used to treat hyperthyroidism, but it may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, diarrhea, and stomach discomfort, as well as allergic reactions like skin rashes and itching. When using Angelica sinensis teas for this purpose, it is essential to take precautions to avoid overdose, as excessive consumption can lead to complications like liver damage and interactions with other medications, particularly blood thinners, and to monitor blood pressure closely due to potential hypotension.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, also known as thyme tea, have been traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as thymol and carvacrol, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism, including anxiety, tremors, and rapid heartbeat. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of thymus vulgaris teas help to regulate thyroid function and reduce oxidative stress, making it a potential natural remedy for hyperthyroidism. Regular consumption of thymus vulgaris teas may also help to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve sleep quality, providing overall benefits in managing hyperthyroidism symptoms.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus vulgaris leaves, 1 cup of boiling water, and a strainer.
Rinse the Thymus vulgaris leaves and chop them into small pieces.
Steep the chopped leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Then, strain the mixture into a cup.
Let the tea cool down for a few minutes. You can add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink 1-2 cups of the Thymus vulgaris tea per day, ideally 2-3 times a day, to help manage hyperthyroidism symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Pharmacological research, Thymus vulgaris teas for hyperthyroidism do not have any direct relation to the provided study as it is about Moringa oleifera aqueous leaf extract.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus vulgaris teas can be used to treat hyperthyroidism, but possible side effects include allergic reactions, digestive issues, and interactions with other medications that may worsen thyroid problems. When using Thymus vulgaris teas to treat hyperthyroidism, take precautions to monitor your thyroid hormone levels and blood pressure closely, and be aware that excessive consumption may lead to thyroid suppression, so it's essential to use them in moderation and adjust your dosage gradually.

preparations.herb_desc: Hypericum perforatum teas, also known as St. John's Wort tea, have been traditionally used to treat hyperthyroidism due to their adaptogenic properties that help to balance the body's hormonal system. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to inhibit the conversion of T4 to T3, a process that is often overactive in individuals with hyperthyroidism. By reducing the levels of T3, Hypericum perforatum teas can help to alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism, such as rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and nervousness. The benefits of using Hypericum perforatum teas to treat hyperthyroidism include natural and non-invasive relief from symptoms, as well as the potential to prevent long-term thyroid damage.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Hypericum perforatum leaves. Fresh leaves are preferred, but dried leaves work too.
Use a tablespoon to measure 1-2 tablespoons of the leaves. Some people prefer more, but start with a small amount.
Place the measured leaves in a clean glass cup or teapot. If using fresh leaves, you can bruise them with a spoon to release their oils.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the leaves. Let it steep for 5-10 minutes, depending on your preference for strength.
Strain the tea into a separate cup and discard the leaves. Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage hyperthyroidism symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Hypericum perforatum teas, which have a high antioxidant capacity (TEAC 3.75+/-0.14 mM) and phenolic compounds content (QE 881.93+/-6.68 microM), may have potential health benefits, but there is no information provided in the study about their specific use for hyperthyroidism.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hypericum perforatum teas can be used to treat hyperthyroidism, but they may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal issues, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Hypericum perforatum teas to treat hyperthyroidism, it is essential to note that prolonged use can lead to interactions with other medications, and it is recommended to start with low doses and gradually increase the amount as needed, also being mindful of the timing of consumption in relation to meals.

preparations.herb_desc: Urtica dioica teas have been traditionally used to treat hyperthyroidism, an overactive thyroid condition characterized by an excessive production of thyroid hormones. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Urtica dioica teas help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with hyperthyroidism, promoting a balanced thyroid function. The bioactive constituents of Urtica dioica teas, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, have been found to inhibit the production of thyroid hormones, thus helping to alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism. Drinking Urtica dioica teas may help to regulate thyroid function, alleviate symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and anxiety, and promote overall well-being in individuals suffering from hyperthyroidism.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Urtica dioica leaves. You can buy them from a store or grow them yourself.
Measure 1 tablespoon of Urtica dioica leaves and put them in a clean tea infuser.
Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or pot. Let it cool for 1-2 minutes.
Place the tea infuser in the cup of water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and drink it 2-3 times a day. You can add honey or lemon if you like.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Urtica dioica teas can be used to treat hyperthyroidism, but it may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications, particularly blood thinners and diuretics. When using Urtica dioica teas to treat this ailment, precautions should be taken to avoid overconsumption, which may lead to excessive potassium loss, and to monitor blood pressure and potassium levels closely, especially in individuals with pre-existing kidney or heart conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas, also known as lemon balm tea, have been traditionally used to treat hyperthyroidism due to their calming and soothing properties. The antispasmodic and anti-anxiety effects of Melissa officinalis teas help to alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism, such as palpitations and tremors. The bioactive constituents, including rosmarinic acid, apigenin, and luteolin, in Melissa officinalis teas possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to regulate thyroid function and reduce oxidative stress. Regular consumption of Melissa officinalis teas has been reported to provide relief from hyperthyroidism symptoms, promote relaxation, and improve overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of fresh Melissa officinalis leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Add the Melissa officinalis leaves to the cooled water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, preferably before meals.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Hormone and metabolic research, Melissa officinalis teas for hyperthyroidism may be beneficial due to their aqueous extracts containing antihormonal components that inhibit the conversion of T4 to T3.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be used to treat hyperthyroidism, but possible side effects may include increased risk of bleeding due to its ability to lower blood pressure and heart rate, and in severe cases, it may exacerbate hypothyroidism in individuals who have pre-existing thyroid issues. Precautions should be taken when consuming Melissa officinalis teas, including avoiding it if you have a history of bleeding disorders or if you're taking medications that thin the blood, as it may interact with these substances and worsen your condition.

preparations.herb_desc: Cynara scolymus teas, derived from the artichoke plant, have been used in traditional medicine to help treat hyperthyroidism. The properties of this herbal preparation, including its ability to inhibit the conversion of T4 to T3 and its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, help to reduce thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism. The bioactive constituents of Cynara scolymus teas, such as cynarin and silymarin, have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-thyroid activities, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Regular consumption of Cynara scolymus teas may help to regulate thyroid function, reduce symptoms of hyperthyroidism, and provide relief from anxiety, weight loss, and palpitations.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Cynara scolymus (artichoke) leaves.
Measure 250ml of boiling water in a cup.
Add 2-3 teaspoons of Cynara scolymus leaves to the boiling water.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into another cup.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea, 2-3 times a day, under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cynara scolymus teas can be used to treat hyperthyroidism, but it may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, diarrhea, and stomach cramps, as well as allergic reactions like skin rashes and itching. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation, including starting with a low dose and gradually increasing it to prevent interactions with other medications and worsening of the condition, as well as monitoring liver function and kidney health for any signs of damage.

intro_study: According to "International journal of biological macromolecules", teas for hyperthyroidism may offer a protective and effective alternative treatment, containing antithyroid compounds that can restore thyroid function to normal.
